source.title: Risk prediction and aversion by anterior cingulate cortex

confidence_rationale: Replicates and extends the error-likelihood account: ACC encodes error likelihood and predicted error magnitude (the product = expected risk). Supports the framing that dACC operates as a downstream consumer of upstream predicted-state estimates -- if those estimates are wrong, dACC has nothing legitimate to weight by. Older fMRI study, indirect for the V_s/MECH-269b mechanism specifically; included as a foundational anchor rather than a strong test.
failure_signatures: ACC's behavioural-control signal is a function of upstream predicted error likelihood and magnitude; if those upstream predictions are stale or non-credibly anchored, the ACC signal will track the stale prediction faithfully and produce no behavioural revision -- consistent with Q-040's wired-but-inert hypothesis.

mapping.source_claim_statement: ACC activity scales with the product of error likelihood and predicted error magnitude (expected risk), validating a computational model in which ACC functions as a downstream consumer of upstream predictions about behavioural outcomes.
mapping.ree_translation: If REE's dACC adaptive-control output (SD-032b) computes something analogous to expected risk -- a function of upstream predictions about harm-likelihood and harm-magnitude -- then the integrity of those upstream predictions matters for whether the dACC signal can drive policy revision. Q-040's prediction is that with V_s gating restricted to the hippocampal proposer (MECH-269), upstream harm-magnitude predictions from E2 are stale, dACC's risk computation is internally consistent but anchored to the wrong estimate, and behaviour does not revise.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Brown and Braver's manipulation varies error likelihood through task design, not through perturbation of upstream state. The supporting inference is purely structural: if ACC computes f(upstream_predictions), then bad upstream predictions produce bad downstream signals. This is mathematically obvious but does not give us any traction on whether V_s specifically is the relevant upstream gate. Older paper (2007), fMRI BOLD only, no causal manipulation.
mapping.source_context: Human fMRI; incentive-change signal task; modelling ACC activity as a function of error likelihood and predicted error consequence magnitude; replication of an earlier computational-model prediction.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality moderate-to-good for its era; the error-likelihood/expected-risk framework remains influential but has been largely subsumed by later work. Mapping fidelity is the limiting factor: this entry supports the *general structural claim* that ACC needs credible upstream predictions, but does not directly bear on the V_s/MECH-269b mechanism. Included as foundational/anchor evidence; would not on its own move the needle on Q-040.
